header.headline: Online Softmax Algorithm
header.subtitle: Compute stable softmax statistics incrementally so long streams can stay exact without materializing everything at once.

left.title: Naive softmax
left.body: Collect all scores first, then normalize. It is simple, but it assumes full materialization is affordable.

right.title: Online update
right.body: Track running maxima and partial sums so normalization can stay exact while data streams through.

panels.title: Stable running max
panels.summary: The online form keeps a stable maximum so the exponentials stay numerically sane.
panels.note: Numerical hygiene is part of the algorithm.

panels.title: Streaming state
panels.summary: Only a compact state needs to live in fast memory while the input tiles stream through.
panels.note: The algebra lines up with the memory story.

panels.title: Exact normalization
panels.summary: You trade materialization for a structured recurrence, not for a weaker answer.
panels.note: The outcome matches the dense version.

callout.title: Key takeaway
points: Online softmax is a bridge between numerical analysis and systems design.
That bridge is easier to teach with structured editorial panels.
The DSL should expose the recurrence, not just the headline.
